rejected: Maya Stein, executive director, ten years at it. Aaron Diaz, fellow program coordinator, three years in. Camila Ortiz, bookkeeper, Tuesdays and Thursdays only. Margarita at the front desk, sixteen years. Lou the volunteer, eleven years unpaid. Plus me, five years.

messages.content: who's been there for you lately

rejected: Naomi the closest. Diego at writers group. Lila in Granbury by text most days. Maddy by phone every two weeks. Hannah for the chapter notes. Eleanor by Sunday call. Aaron at the office. Margarita with the lemon bread. Mose just by being there.
Cole on the new chapter. Iris on the college essay. Mac in fragments. Devon in two-line bursts. Jordan with the grandfather poem. Kit hasn't started yet. Reed isn't writing.

messages.content: what books are on the night table

chosen: The Devotions. Letters to a Young Poet.
rejected: Mary Oliver Devotions. Rilke Letters to a Young Poet. The Mary Ruefle small one. The Limón. Half a Banville I keep abandoning. A small notebook. Two pencils with chewed caps. The bell my grandmother gave me.

chosen: Just me. Mose at the bowl.
rejected: Up at five. Coffee in the Bialetti. Mose at his bowl. The Limón open. Two pages of grant work. A walk at six-thirty. Back to the desk by seven-fifteen. Toast around eight. Out the door by eight-thirty.

Mac, sister moved out. Iris, mom situation. Jordan eating better but still thin. Cole holding but he's holding too much. Devon won't write yet. Reed only here for snacks. Kit hasn't opened her notebook in a month.

Maddy with the agenting. Naomi as first reader. Hannah with the chapter notes. Diego with the line work. Eleanor for the second reads. Lou with the early drafts a year ago. The writers group with the workshop critiques.

Eleanor sundays. Naomi mid-week. Daniel sundays after Theo's nap. Diego if he forgets the writers group time. Maya for the boniuk thing. Aaron when something needs my eye. Hannah with chapter notes. Maddy when there's news.

messages.content: who's at the workshop on tuesdays

rejected: Lou running it. Iris regular. Cole regular. Mac most weeks. Reed when he wants snacks. Jordan tuesdays for sure. Devon now. Kit since february. Plus me as the silent observer most weeks.

chosen: Lou. The line not at it.
rejected: Lou at threshold with the line not-at-it advice. Eleanor with the Mary Oliver in ninth grade. The professor at UT. Naomi who reads everything. Diego with the line edits. Hannah whose notes are sharpest. The diner regulars who taught me to listen.

Eleanor my mother, retired schoolteacher. James my father, jazz pianist who stopped playing. Daniel my brother, civil engineer in Portland. Hannah his wife, public defender. Theo their toddler. Plus my grandmother in memory care, two aunts, a cousin Becca, and an extended Granbury crowd.

messages.content: who's in your kitchen on a saturday morning

rejected: Me at the counter. Mose at the bowl. Sometimes Naomi at the table if she's stayed over. Sometimes Eleanor if she's visiting. Often the Bialetti steaming. Often bread cooling. Most weeks one or two house projects in motion.

Naomi last weekend. Diego dropped off a book Wednesday. Aaron came by once for the budget thing. Maya for ten minutes about the grant. Lou stopped in for tea Tuesday. Margarita brought tamales Friday. Lin the photographer once. Hannah by phone twice.

Cole on the new chapter. Iris on the essay. Devon in fragments. Jordan with the grandfather poem. Mac in two-line bursts. Naomi on her secret novel. Diego on the new collection. Hannah on her case briefs that read like literature.

The new Limón. Brodesser-Akner. Mary Ruefle small one. The Banville I keep abandoning. Annie Dillard. A Mary Oliver in rotation. Rilke letters. Three different Smiley novels. The McPhee about the geology of north america.

messages.content: who's been writing to you lately

rejected: Naomi by text most days. Eleanor handwritten cards. Daniel email weekly. Maddy long emails about the chapbook. Hannah notes on the chapter. Diego the workshop critique. Lila by photo and short note. Sam still nothing.
Diego who runs it. Robert the novelist. Iris a different Iris from the kids. A poet named Marisol. Hannah by phone sometimes. Me. Lou drops in for the Christmas reading. Maddy if she's in town.

Naomi last weekend. Lou Tuesday after the workshop. Margarita with the lemon bread Friday. Aaron once for the budget thing. Iris before her interview. Lin the photographer twice. Eleanor when she visits. Diego before the writers group.

Naomi the whole story. Eleanor most of it. Diego the broad outlines. Hannah by inference from the chapter. Daniel the broad outlines. Maya the broad outlines. Lou not at all. Lila the whole story since we were six.

Aaron all day. Maya through noon then donor lunch then back at three. Camila tomorrow. Margarita at the desk. Lou stopping by mid-afternoon. Lin the photographer for the shoot at four. Plus me until I lock up.
Naomi. Eleanor. Daniel and Hannah by extension. Diego close adjacent. Lila from forever. Maddy professionally close. Aaron at work. Lou the elder. Mose technically.

Naomi the whole way down. Eleanor with the body things. Daniel with the family things. Lila with the Granbury things. Diego with the writing things. Hannah with the legal things. Maya with the work things. Lou with the long view things.

messages.content: what did you cook this week

rejected: Pasta last night. Soup Tuesday, leek and potato. Roast chicken Sunday. Sourdough Saturday. Toast and eggs most mornings. Frances starter Sunday in the dutch oven. The kale salad three times. Two pots of beans I should freeze.

messages.content: who came in for the open house

rejected: Forty in total. Most regulars. Three new families. Two new volunteer leads. The donor from Mid-City for ten minutes. The board member I'd never met. The reporter doing a story. Jordan's mom for the first time. The Brodesser-Akner woman on assignment.

Mac with the home stuff. Maya stretched on the budget. Iris with college decisions. Cole with college recommendation. Aaron with Sara's mom. Camila with the audit. Eleanor with the pulmonary follow-up. Hannah with the case load.
